title: Convertitore di Lunghezza
description: Converti istantaneamente tra tutte le unità di lunghezza: metri, piedi, pollici, km, miglia, centimetri, yard e altro.

faq.title: Domande Frequenti
faq.q1: Quanti piedi ci sono in un metro?
faq.a1: 1 metro = 3,28084 piedi. Viceversa, 1 piede = 0,3048 metri (esattamente per definizione). Per una conversione rapida mentale: moltiplica i metri per 3,28 per ottenere i piedi approssimativi.
faq.q2: Come si convertono i pollici in centimetri?
faq.a2: 1 pollice = 2,54 cm (esattamente per definizione). Per convertire pollici in cm, moltiplica per 2,54. Per convertire cm in pollici, dividi per 2,54. Esempio: 72 pollici (altezza) = 72 × 2,54 = 182,88 cm.
faq.q3: Quante miglia ci sono in un chilometro?
faq.a3: 1 km = 0,621371 miglia. 1 miglio = 1,60934 km. Per una conversione rapida: 10 km ≈ 6,2 miglia. Una maratona è 42,195 km ≈ 26,2 miglia.
faq.q4: Cos'è un yard e come si usa?
faq.a4: 1 yard = 0,9144 metri = 3 piedi = 36 pollici. Usato principalmente negli USA, nel calcio americano (campo di 100 yard = 91,44 m) e in alcuni contesti tessili. Per la vita quotidiana europea, è meno comune dei piedi e pollici.
faq.q5: Come si converte un'altezza da piedi/pollici a cm?
faq.a5: Converti prima tutto in pollici (moltiplicando i piedi per 12, poi aggiungendo i pollici rimanenti), poi moltiplica per 2,54. Es: 5 piedi 10 pollici = (5×12+10) × 2,54 = 70 × 2,54 = 177,8 cm.
faq.q6: Cos'è un nanometro e quando si usa?
faq.a6: 1 nanometro = 10⁻⁹ metri (un miliardesimo di metro). Usato in nanotecnologia, fisica delle particelle, lunghezze d'onda della luce visibile (400-700 nm), transistor nei processori moderni (meno di 10 nm). La larghezza di un capello umano è circa 70.000 nm.
faq.q7: Come si misurano le distanze astronomiche?
faq.a7: Per distanze astronomiche si usano unità speciali: Unità Astronomica (UA) = distanza Terra-Sole ≈ 150 milioni di km. Anno luce = distanza percorsa dalla luce in 1 anno ≈ 9,46 trilioni di km. Parsec = 3,26 anni luce. La stella più vicina (Proxima Centauri) è a 4,24 anni luce.
faq.q8: Come funzionano i convertitori di lunghezza nelle app di navigazione?
faq.a8: Le app di navigazione come Google Maps o Apple Maps usano internamente il sistema metrico (metri/km) e convertono per la visualizzazione in base alle preferenze regionali dell'utente (km in Europa, miglia negli USA). Le distanze GPS sono calcolate in gradi di latitudine/longitudine poi convertite in km o miglia.

content.whatIs:
A length converter translates measurements between the full range of length units — from the metric SI system (nanometers to kilometers), to imperial and U.S. customary units (inches, feet, yards, miles), to specialized units used in navigation (nautical miles), astronomy (light-years, astronomical units), and typography (points, picas). Enter a value in any unit and the converter instantly produces the equivalent in any other unit using exact or high-precision conversion factors.

Length measurement is humanity's oldest metrological practice. Ancient civilizations used body-based units — the cubit (forearm length, approximately 45–52 cm), the foot, the palm, the fathom (arm span). These varied by region, era, and even royal decree, creating commercial chaos in trade between nations. The French Revolution's Système International d'Unités (1795) introduced the meter as a universal standard: one ten-millionth of the distance from the equator to the North Pole along the Paris meridian. Today the meter is defined with far greater precision in terms of the speed of light, ensuring reproducibility anywhere in the universe.

Despite the global adoption of the metric system by almost every country, length conversions remain genuinely necessary in the modern world. The United States uses a mix of imperial and metric depending on context (miles on road signs, centimeters in medicine). The UK uses miles for road distances but meters for athletics. International aviation uses feet for altitude and nautical miles for distance. Scientific papers use SI units while construction in the U.S. uses feet and inches. This persistent pluralism of measurement systems makes a reliable, precise length converter an everyday practical tool.
content.formula:
Core SI metric conversions (exact by definition):
1 kilometer (km) = 1,000 meters (m)
1 meter (m) = 100 centimeters (cm) = 1,000 millimeters (mm)
1 centimeter = 10 millimeters
1 millimeter = 1,000 micrometers (μm) = 1,000,000 nanometers (nm)

Imperial / U.S. customary (exact):
1 mile = 5,280 feet = 1,760 yards
1 yard = 3 feet = 36 inches
1 foot = 12 inches
1 inch = 25.4 mm (exact, defined 1959)

Key cross-system conversions:
1 meter = 3.28084 feet = 39.3701 inches
1 kilometer = 0.621371 miles
1 mile = 1.609344 kilometers
1 inch = 2.54 centimeters
1 foot = 0.3048 meters (exact)
1 yard = 0.9144 meters (exact)

Specialized units:
1 nautical mile = 1,852 meters = 1.15078 statute miles
1 astronomical unit (AU) = 149,597,870,700 meters
1 light-year = 9.4607×10¹⁵ meters
1 parsec = 3.0857×10¹⁶ meters = 3.2616 light-years
content.example:
Example 1 — Running distances:
5 kilometers = 5 × 0.621371 = 3.107 miles (standard 5K race)
10 kilometers = 6.214 miles
Half marathon = 21.0975 km = 13.109 miles
Full marathon = 42.195 km = 26.219 miles

Example 2 — Human height conversion:
5 feet 10 inches = (5 × 12 + 10) = 70 inches = 70 × 2.54 = 177.8 cm = 1.778 m
180 cm = 180 / 2.54 = 70.87 inches = 5 feet 10.87 inches

Example 3 — Construction measurement:
15 feet 6 inches to meters:
15.5 feet × 0.3048 = 4.724 meters
Room of 12 ft × 16 ft = 144 sq ft = 13.38 m² (not a length converter calculation, but related)

Example 4 — Travel distance:
New York to Los Angeles: approximately 2,790 miles
2,790 × 1.60934 = 4,490 kilometers
Equivalent: 4,490,000 meters = 449,000 centimeters = 14,730,000 feet
content.howItWorks_1: Enter the numerical value you want to convert and select the source unit from the 'From' dropdown. The converter supports all major metric units (nanometer through kilometer), imperial units (inch, foot, yard, mile), and specialized units including nautical mile, astronomical unit, and light-year.
content.howItWorks_2: Select the target unit from the 'To' dropdown. All conversions use exact or high-precision conversion factors defined by international standards (SI, the 1959 International Agreement for inch/foot/yard, and the international nautical mile agreement of 1929).
content.howItWorks_3: The result appears instantly. For very large or very small converted values, the result is displayed in both standard decimal notation and scientific notation to ensure readability. You can swap the From and To units to perform the reverse conversion.
content.whyItMatters:
The practical cost of unit conversion errors throughout history has been severe. The most famous is the 1999 Mars Climate Orbiter loss — NASA lost a $327 million spacecraft because one engineering team used imperial units (pound-force seconds) while another used metric units (newton-seconds) in thrust calculations. The spacecraft entered Mars's atmosphere at the wrong angle and was destroyed. The investigation found the error was a simple failure to convert units — a lesson that should have been learned centuries earlier but continues to create problems wherever unit systems mix.

In everyday professional contexts, unit conversion errors cause costly mistakes with surprising frequency. Pharmaceutical dosing errors occur when mg/kg doses are not properly converted for patient weight in different measurement systems. Construction and manufacturing errors arise from imperial-to-metric specification translation mistakes. International shipping and logistics must convert between weight, volume, and dimensional length units across systems. Medical imaging uses centimeters; surgical tools are often sized in inches; patient height is recorded in feet in the U.S. and meters elsewhere. These simultaneous system requirements create daily conversion needs for healthcare professionals.

For consumers, length conversion matters most in international shopping (clothing sizes between U.S., UK, and European systems), real estate (property dimensions in feet vs. meters), fitness (race distances in km vs. miles), and travel (road speeds in mph vs. km/h). The ability to quickly and accurately convert removes friction from international commerce and ensures that a '100-meter sprint' and a '100-yard dash' are understood as different events (100 yards = 91.44 meters).
content.limitations:
This converter uses exact conversion factors for all units that have exact definitions (meter, foot, inch, yard, mile, nautical mile, and all SI prefixed units), and high-precision factors for units like the astronomical unit and light-year that are defined with high but not infinite precision.

The converter does not handle surveying units still used in U.S. land records: the survey foot (1 survey foot = 1200/3937 meters exactly, slightly different from the international foot at 0.3048 meters), the rod (16.5 survey feet), the chain (66 survey feet = 4 rods), or the furlong (10 chains). These units appear in U.S. property deeds and legal land descriptions, particularly for land surveyed before 1959. The difference between survey and international feet accumulates to about 3 millimeters per mile — small but legally significant in property boundaries.

For astronomy applications, note that the light-year is often approximate in conversion tools: the exact value depends on the definition of the Julian year (365.25 days × 24 hours × 60 minutes × 60 seconds × 299,792,458 m/s = 9.4607304725808 × 10¹⁵ meters). Parsec is defined as exactly 648,000/π astronomical units, making its meter value dependent on the exact AU definition. For precision astronomical calculations, these distinctions matter.
content.tips_1: For quick mental conversion between km and miles, multiply km by 0.6 (for a fast rough estimate) or by 5/8 (exact: 0.625, about 2.5% too high). The Fibonacci approximation is more accurate: 5 miles ≈ 8 km, 8 ≈ 13, 13 ≈ 21, 21 ≈ 34. For speed limits: 50 km/h ≈ 31 mph, 100 km/h ≈ 62 mph, 130 km/h ≈ 81 mph.
content.tips_2: When converting feet and inches to decimal feet for calculation, use: decimal feet = feet + (inches / 12). Then convert to meters: meters = decimal feet × 0.3048. Example: 5'9" = 5 + 9/12 = 5.75 feet = 5.75 × 0.3048 = 1.7526 meters. Going the other way: 1.80 m / 0.3048 = 5.906 feet = 5 feet + 0.906 × 12 = 5 feet 10.87 inches.
content.tips_3: For international clothing and shoe purchases, build a personal reference: know your height in centimeters (multiply feet×30.48 + inches×2.54), your inseam in centimeters, and your foot length in centimeters. European sizing uses centimeters directly for height and foot length. Having these converted measurements ready prevents the most common international sizing mistake.
content.tips_4: In construction, always verify whether drawings use feet-inches or decimal feet before performing calculations. Architectural drawings in the U.S. typically use feet-inches notation (5'-6"), while engineering drawings often use decimal feet (5.5'). Mixing these without conversion is a very common source of measurement error on construction sites. When in doubt, convert everything to millimeters — the international construction standard used everywhere except the U.S.
